title: Slideshow Death Songs — friend group funeral-song picks, Jul 2025
html:
TL;DR

  • A list of death-song picks for each member of the core friend group — likely sourced from a real conversation. Each person picks the song that should play at their funeral slideshow.
  • The list:
    • Valeria — It's the Climb.
    • Yahir — Your Sweet Love by Lee Hazlewood.
    • Riley Bonney — Come Back to Earth by Mac Miller.
    • Kai Perez — White Trash Heroes / Goodbye Angels by Chili Peppers.
    • Andrew Pruhliere — Play That Funky Music White Boy.
    • Kimiya — Big Poppa by [Notorious BIG / "snoop dawg"] / Age Ye Rooz (Iranian).
    • Dariush Samus — (no song attributed).

Key data point

Yahir's funeral song = Your Sweet Love by Lee Hazlewood. Riley's = Come Back to Earth by Mac Miller.

Why it matters in the graph

  • Confirms full names for several friends previously partial: Andrew Pruhliere, Riley Bonney, Kai Perez (full surname), Kimiya, Dariush Samus.
  • The death-song-as-self-portrait conceit pairs with 2025 05 11 boys quiz (trivia about each other) and 2025 05 09 initiation to chelympics (each person assigned a totem-song for the games) — three different formats of friend-group mythology-building.
  • The mortality-frame links to 2025 11 22 cool automations (the death-detection-and-final-message n8n automation) — Yahir's friend group jokes/talks about death often enough that it generates ideas.
  • Come Back to Earth (Mac Miller, who died young) as Riley's pick is a real-flag worth tracking; in the 2024 04 27 sam breakup script / Yahir-emotional landscape this picks a lane.

Open questions

  • Why does Yahir pick Your Sweet Love — Lee Hazlewood is unusual taste; gloss elsewhere?
  • Is Dariush Samus a real friend or pseudonym? Dariush + Iranian-music shows up in Kimiya's picks — possibly the same family/friend group?
